description_long: This object is a corded phone mounted on a pedestal, resembling a public payphone. The main body is rectangular with a recessed front panel housing a handset attached by a cord, and a cradle for resting the handset. The surface is covered with a highly unusual texture that appears to be a photographic print of a human face, giving it a surreal and artistic appearance. The sides and back are flat, with the face image wrapping around, and the base is a simple rectangular column attached to a square platform for stability. The cord is visibly attached to the handset and base, and there are no visible buttons or rotary dial on the front panel. The object appears to be made primarily of plastic or metal, with some internal air space and wiring.
description: A pedestal-mounted corded phone with a surreal human face texture, featuring a handset on a cord and a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the recessed panel, handset with cord, and the face texture most prominently displayed.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the flat side with the face texture and the cord trailing from the handset.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the phone, fully covered in the face texture, with no visible interactive features.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with the face texture and the cord visible.
